title: Restaurant Menu Redesign
brief: Project Overview We are a ramen and sushi restaurant called House of Umami. We are updating our existing printed menu and need a professional designer to revise the layout while incorporating new menu items and pricing updates. The goal is not a full redesign, but rather an updated, clean version of our existing menu that fits additional items without feeling crowded, especially on the ramen section. Our current menu is a tri-fold printed menu (22.5” x 12” unfolded) used for dine-in customers. Scope of Work The designer will: Update our existing menu design Insert new menu items Remove discontinued items Update prices Adjust categories where needed Optimize the layout Ensure all content fits cleanly within the tri-fold format Improve readability and flow Avoid overcrowding (especially in the ramen section) Reorganize sections if necessary Current menu sections include: Small Plates Sushi Bowls Artisan Ramen Donburi Rice Bowls Sushi Rolls Nigiri / Sashimi Kids Desserts Solve the biggest layout challenge We are adding several ramen items and a Wagyu section. The designer should thoughtfully reorganize the ramen area so it remains clear and easy to read. Maintain our current brand style The current menu has: Dark background Food photography Red accent elements Japanese ramen/sushi aesthetic We want the same general vibe, but cleaner and more professional. Deliverables Final deliverables should include: • Print-ready PDF (CMYK, 300 DPI) • Editable source file (Adobe Illustrator o
